tasks.task_name: Rewrite plan-spec SKILL.md to 4-phase interview-driven workflow
tasks.goal: The plan-spec SKILL.md contains exactly 4 phases (Discovery, FR Drafting, Scenarios+Tests, Quality Gate), uses interview pattern for discovery, includes revision mode with YAML parsing and stall detection, and has 7 quality checks.

outputs.name: rewritten-skill
outputs.constraints: 4 phases, interview pattern, 7 quality checks, revision mode with YAML parsing

acceptance: SKILL.md contains exactly 4 phase headings: Discovery, FR Drafting (or Codebase Scouting + FR Drafting), Scenarios + Tests (or Behavioral Scenarios + Tests + Traceability), Quality Gate
Discovery phase describes interview pattern without prescriptive question lists
Quality Gate phase lists exactly 7 checks matching the plan specification
Revision mode section describes YAML Issues block parsing, BLOCKER-first ordering, stall detection, and 3-round hard cap
Specificity test 'Could a different Claude execute this without asking clarifying questions?' appears in the document
Weasel-word prohibited list is referenced or included
No references to user stories, holdout scenarios, test datasets, or behavioral contracts remain

depends_on.reason: First task, requires no prior task output

constraints: Must not break the plan-spec invocation interface

tasks.task_name: Rewrite plan-spec spec-template.md to 14 lean sections
tasks.goal: The spec-template.md contains exactly 14 sections matching the canonical target spec structure, with no narrative filler or eliminated sections present.

outputs.name: rewritten-template
outputs.constraints: 14 sections, lean format, no eliminated sections

acceptance: Template contains exactly 14 numbered sections: Header, Implementation Scope, Existing Codebase Context, Terminology, Surface/API Inventory, Data Model Changes, Functional Requirements, API/Schema Contracts, Error Contract, Behavioral Scenarios, Testing Requirements, Success Criteria, Traceability Matrix, Task Decomposition Guidance
Sections 3-6, 8-9, 14 are marked as conditional (not always required)
Surface/API Inventory includes 'Deferred From This Spec' subsection
Functional Requirements section uses FR-NNN numbering with MUST/SHOULD/MAY
Behavioral Scenarios use 3 categories: Happy Path, Error Path, Edge Case
Testing Requirements section uses three bulleted lists (Unit, Integration, E2E Smoke), not numbered test catalogs
No user stories, behavioral contract, holdout, test datasets, or assumptions sections remain
constraints: Template sections must align with the phases described in the rewritten SKILL.md

tasks.task_name: Rewrite plan-spec bdd-template.md to lean 3-category FR-traced format
tasks.goal: The bdd-template.md traces scenarios to FR-NNN IDs (not user stories), uses 3 categories (Happy/Error/Edge), and omits Scenario Outlines, Background blocks, and Examples tables.

outputs.name: rewritten-bdd
outputs.constraints: FR-traced, 3 categories, no Outlines/Background/Examples

acceptance: All 'Traces to' references use FR-NNN format, not User Story N
Exactly 3 categories are defined: Happy Path, Error Path, Edge Case (no Alternate category)
No Scenario Outline, Background block, or Examples table sections exist in the template
3-4 key anti-patterns are retained
Naming conventions section is retained in shortened form
constraints: Must be consistent with the scenario format referenced in spec-template.md

tasks.goal: The file ~/.claude/skills/plan-spec/test-dataset-template.md no longer exists on disk.

outputs.name: deleted-confirmation
outputs.constraints: File no longer exists

acceptance: The file ~/.claude/skills/plan-spec/test-dataset-template.md does not exist
No other files in ~/.claude/skills/plan-spec/ reference test-dataset-template.md

constraints.reason: Simple file deletion with no behavioral constraints

tasks.task_name: Rewrite plan-spec sample-output.md as password-reset spec in lean format
tasks.goal: The sample-output.md demonstrates all 14 applicable sections of the new spec template using a password-reset feature example at multi-file feature detail level (300-800 words plus tests/contracts).

outputs.name: sample-spec
outputs.constraints: Password-reset spec, all applicable sections, 300-800 words core + tests/contracts

acceptance: Sample uses a password-reset feature as the example domain
All 14 section headings from spec-template.md are present (conditional sections included with example content or marked N/A)
FRs use FR-NNN numbering with MUST/SHOULD/MAY classification
Behavioral scenarios trace to FR-IDs and use 3 categories (Happy Path, Error Path, Edge Case)
Testing requirements use three bulleted lists (Unit, Integration, E2E Smoke)
Traceability matrix uses rolled-up FR-range per row format
No user stories, holdout scenarios, test datasets, or behavioral contracts appear
constraints: Must serve as a realistic reference example that agents can follow

tasks.task_name: Rewrite grill-spec SKILL.md with 8 lenses, 2 alignment checks, and quality gates
tasks.goal: The grill-spec SKILL.md defines a 3-tier review structure (8 adversarial lenses, 2 alignment checks, quality gates), uses 3 severity levels (BLOCKER/WARNING/INFO), drops STRIDE matrix and variant classification, and requires YAML issues block output.

outputs.name: rewritten-skill
outputs.constraints: 3-tier structure, 3 severity levels, YAML issues block, no STRIDE matrix

acceptance: SKILL.md defines 8 adversarial lenses (Ambiguity, Incompleteness, Inconsistency, Infeasibility, Insecurity, Inoperability, Incorrectness, Overcomplexity)
SKILL.md defines 2 alignment checks (Scope Alignment, Codebase Fit)

Quality gates section lists 7 anti-bloat checks (user-story narrative, deleted section reappeared, numbered test catalog, weasel words, non-falsifiable criterion, specificity failure, deferred item lacks reason)
Severity levels are exactly BLOCKER, WARNING, INFO (no CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, OBSERVATION)
Verdict mapping is defined: any BLOCKER -> BLOCK, any WARNING (no BLOCKER) -> REVISE, only INFO -> PASS
Input classification uses FR-NNN + Behavioral Scenarios heading detection, not variant-A/B/C
YAML issues block format is specified with id, dimension, severity, affected, description, fix_hint fields
No STRIDE matrix table rendering instruction exists
Max 3 revision cycles with stall detection is specified

depends_on.reason: Independent of plan-spec changes, can be worked in parallel

constraints: Must preserve all 8 adversarial lenses as the core innovation

tasks.task_name: Update grill-spec review-constitution.md with alignment checks and anti-bloat definitions
tasks.goal: The review-constitution.md contains all 8 original lens principle tables plus new Scope Alignment (SCP-01 to SCP-03), Codebase Fit (FIT-01 to FIT-03), weasel-word prohibited list, specificity test definition, anti-bloat check definitions, and updated completeness check.

outputs.name: updated-constitution
outputs.constraints: Original 8 lenses + 6 new sections

acceptance: All 8 original lens principle tables (AMB-01 through CPX-10) are preserved unchanged
Scope Alignment principles SCP-01, SCP-02, SCP-03 are present with correct anti-patterns
Codebase Fit principles FIT-01, FIT-02, FIT-03 are present with correct anti-patterns

Weasel-word prohibited list contains all 12 phrases: v1, v2, simplified version, static for now, hardcoded for now, future enhancement, placeholder, basic version, minimal implementation, will be wired later, dynamic in future phase, skip for now
Specificity test definition quotes 'Could a different Claude instance execute this requirement without asking clarifying questions?'
7 anti-bloat check definitions with detection patterns are present
Review Completeness Check drops STRIDE coverage requirement and adds anti-bloat and alignment check requirements
constraints: Must not modify existing lens principle tables

tasks.task_name: Rewrite grill-spec report-template.md with YAML issues block and simplified structure
tasks.goal: The report-template.md includes YAML issues block as a required section, uses 3-tier severity (BLOCKER/WARNING/INFO), has single adaptive structural integrity section, and omits STRIDE matrix, dataset gaps, and variant tables.

outputs.name: rewritten-template
outputs.constraints: YAML issues block, 3-tier severity, no STRIDE matrix/dataset gaps/variant tables

acceptance: Template contains these sections: Executive Summary, Findings by severity, Structural Integrity (single adaptive), Test Coverage Assessment (missing categories only), Unasked Questions, Verdict Rationale + Recommended Next Actions, Issues YAML block
Issues YAML block is a required final section with id/dimension/severity/affected/description/fix_hint fields
Severity labels are BLOCKER, WARNING, INFO throughout the template
No STRIDE Threat Summary table template exists
No Test Coverage Dataset Gaps table template exists
No Variant-A/B/C structural integrity tables exist (replaced by single adaptive section)
constraints: Must produce output parseable by plan-spec --revise mode

tasks.task_name: Rewrite grill-spec sample-review.md demonstrating new review format
tasks.goal: The sample-review.md demonstrates all 8 lenses, 2 alignment checks, 3-tier severity, anti-bloat findings, and YAML issues block against a lean spec example.

outputs.name: sample-review
outputs.constraints: Demonstrates complete new review format

acceptance: Sample review includes findings from at least 4 of the 8 adversarial lenses
Sample review includes at least 1 finding from each alignment check (Scope Alignment, Codebase Fit)
Sample review includes at least 1 anti-bloat finding from quality gates
All findings use BLOCKER, WARNING, or INFO severity (no CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR/OBSERVATION)
YAML issues block at the end is valid YAML with all required fields (id, dimension, severity, affected, description, fix_hint)
No STRIDE matrix table appears in the sample
Executive summary includes verdict and finding counts
constraints: Must be a realistic example that demonstrates the full review process

tasks.task_name: Implement V11 weasel-word validation rule in semantic.go
tasks.goal: The taskval V11 check detects GSD weasel words (v1, placeholder, basic version, hardcoded for now, future enhancement, will be wired later, simplified version, static for now, minimal implementation, dynamic in future phase, skip for now) in goal and acceptance fields, producing WARNING-severity findings.

outputs.name: v11-check-function
outputs.constraints: checkWeaselWords method on SemanticValidator, WARNING severity

acceptance: Function checkWeaselWords exists on SemanticValidator and is called from ValidateTaskGraph
All 12 weasel phrases from the plan are detected case-insensitively as whole words/phrases
Matches in goal field produce WARNING with rule V11
Matches in acceptance criteria produce WARNING with rule V11
Each finding includes the matched phrase, the field path, and a suggestion to rewrite
go build ./internal/validator/... succeeds with no errors

depends_on.reason: Independent code change to existing Go module

constraints: Must follow existing code patterns in semantic.go (ValidationError struct, AddError method)
Must use regexp word-boundary matching consistent with existing goalForbiddenPatterns approach

tasks.task_name: Implement V12 cross-task output-to-input type contract check in semantic.go
tasks.goal: The taskval V12 check verifies that when task B's input.source textually references task A's output.name, the input.type and output.type are compatible (exact string match or known-compatible pair), producing WARNING-severity findings on mismatch.

inputs.name: task-graph-model
inputs.constraints: TaskGraph with TaskNode containing InputSpec and OutputSpec

outputs.name: v12-check-function
outputs.constraints: checkCrossTaskContracts method on SemanticValidator, WARNING severity

acceptance: Function checkCrossTaskContracts exists on SemanticValidator and is called from ValidateTaskGraph
For each task B input whose source text contains a task A task_id or output name, the types are compared
Exact type string match is considered compatible
Type mismatch produces WARNING with rule V12 including both type strings and the affected tasks
Tasks with no cross-references produce no V12 findings
go build ./internal/validator/... succeeds with no errors

depends_on.reason: Independent code change, no dependency on V11

constraints: Must handle cases where source text does not reference any other task (no false positives)
Must use case-insensitive substring matching for source references

tasks.task_name: Implement V13 granularity heuristic check in semantic.go
tasks.goal: The taskval V13 check produces INFO-severity suggestions when estimate is 'large', when the graph exceeds 20 tasks, or when a single milestone contains more than 8 tasks.

inputs.name: task-graph-model
inputs.constraints: TaskGraph with optional estimate fields and milestones

outputs.name: v13-check-function
outputs.constraints: checkGranularity method on SemanticValidator, INFO severity

acceptance: Function checkGranularity exists on SemanticValidator and is called from ValidateTaskGraph
Task with estimate 'large' produces INFO with rule V13 suggesting splitting
Graph with more than 20 tasks produces INFO with rule V13 suggesting consolidation
Milestone with more than 8 tasks produces INFO with rule V13 suggesting sub-milestones
Graph with 20 or fewer tasks and no large estimates produces no V13 findings
go build ./internal/validator/... succeeds with no errors

depends_on.reason: Independent code change, no dependency on V11 or V12

constraints: Must follow existing code patterns for adding new checks

tasks.task_name: Implement V14 missing dependency link detection in semantic.go
tasks.goal: The taskval V14 check detects when task B's input.source textually references task A (by task_id or output name) but task A is not in task B's depends_on, producing WARNING-severity findings for the missing dependency.

inputs.name: semantic-validator
inputs.constraints: Existing SemanticValidator struct with taskIndex map

outputs.name: v14-check-function
outputs.constraints: checkMissingDependencyLinks method on SemanticValidator, WARNING severity

acceptance: Function checkMissingDependencyLinks exists on SemanticValidator and is called from ValidateTaskGraph
When task B's input source references task A's task_id but task A is not in task B's depends_on, a WARNING with rule V14 is produced
When task B's input source references task A's output name but task A is not in task B's depends_on, a WARNING with rule V14 is produced
When the referenced task is already in depends_on, no V14 finding is produced
Tasks with no cross-references produce no V14 findings
go build ./internal/validator/... succeeds with no errors

depends_on.reason: Independent code change, no dependency on other V-rules

constraints: Must avoid false positives when source text coincidentally contains a substring matching another task_id

tasks.task_name: Add test cases for V11, V12, V13, and V14 validation rules
tasks.goal: The validate_test.go file contains passing test cases that exercise all V11-V14 rules, covering both positive detection and negative (no false positive) scenarios.

outputs.name: test-functions
outputs.constraints: At least 4 test functions covering V11-V14

acceptance: TestWeaselWords function exists and verifies V11 detects weasel phrases in goals and acceptance criteria
TestWeaselWords includes a negative case verifying clean text produces no V11 warnings
TestCrossTaskContracts function exists and verifies V12 detects type mismatches between linked task inputs/outputs
TestGranularity function exists and verifies V13 fires for large estimate, >20 tasks, and >8 tasks per milestone
TestMissingDependencyLinks function exists and verifies V14 detects unreferenced dependencies
go test ./internal/validator/... passes with 0 failures
constraints: Must follow existing test patterns in validate_test.go

tasks.task_name: Create invalid_weasel_words.json and invalid_cross_contracts.json test fixtures
tasks.goal: Two new example JSON files exist that trigger V11 and V12/V14 warnings respectively when validated by taskval.

inputs.name: v12-v14-rules
inputs.constraints: V12 cross-contract and V14 missing link detection

outputs.name: weasel-fixture
outputs.constraints: Valid task graph structure that triggers V11

outputs.name: contracts-fixture
outputs.constraints: Valid task graph structure that triggers V12 and V14

acceptance: examples/invalid_weasel_words.json is valid JSON conforming to task_graph schema structure
Running taskval --mode=graph on invalid_weasel_words.json produces at least 1 V11 WARNING
examples/invalid_cross_contracts.json is valid JSON conforming to task_graph schema structure
Running taskval --mode=graph on invalid_cross_contracts.json produces at least 1 V12 WARNING and at least 1 V14 WARNING
Running taskval --mode=graph on existing valid examples produces no V11, V12, V13, or V14 errors (no false positives)
constraints: Must be structurally valid task graphs (pass schema validation) to isolate semantic rule testing

tasks.task_name: Update taskify SKILL.md, task-writing-guide.md, and spec-reference.md with V11-V14 guidance
tasks.goal: The three taskify documentation files contain GSD-informed guidance covering specificity test, weasel-word awareness, cross-task contracts, granularity guidance, and V11-V14 rule references.

outputs.name: updated-skill
outputs.constraints: Added GSD-informed guidance

outputs.name: updated-guide
outputs.constraints: Added 3 new sections

outputs.name: updated-reference
outputs.constraints: Added V11-V14 to rules table

acceptance: SKILL.md contains specificity test guidance with the quoted question
SKILL.md lists weasel-word prohibited phrases and explains they trigger V11
SKILL.md describes cross-task contract requirements and mentions V12/V14
SKILL.md includes granularity guidance about splitting large estimates and merging beyond 20 tasks
task-writing-guide.md contains 'Prohibited scope-reduction language' section with weasel-word list and rewrite examples
task-writing-guide.md contains 'Cross-task contracts' section with input/output reference example
task-writing-guide.md contains 'Granularity signals' section with split/merge criteria
spec-reference.md Validation Rules Reference table includes V11, V12, V13, V14 with severity, description, pass example, and fail example

tasks.task_name: Rebuild taskval binary and run full regression validation
tasks.goal: The taskval binary is rebuilt with V11-V14 rules, all tests pass, and existing valid examples produce no false positives from the new rules.

inputs.name: all-code-changes
inputs.constraints: semantic.go with V11-V14, validate_test.go with new tests

outputs.name: taskval-binary
outputs.constraints: Built from updated source

outputs.name: test-results
outputs.constraints: All tests pass

acceptance: go test ./internal/validator/... passes with 0 failures
go build -o taskval ./cmd/taskval/ succeeds
taskval --mode=graph examples/valid_task_graph.json produces no V11, V12, V13, or V14 findings
taskval --mode=graph examples/invalid_weasel_words.json produces V11 warnings
taskval --mode=graph examples/invalid_cross_contracts.json produces V12 and V14 warnings
taskval --mode=task examples/valid_single_task.json produces no new warnings from V11-V14
